message:
perf(ft-kernel-cpu): pairwise summation in var_dim / std_dim

Companion to commits 69fce3d (sum/mean) and cfde2de (norm) in the
pairwise-summation precision arc. The variance kernel was already
two-pass (which avoids the cancellation-prone E[X²] - E[X]² formula),
but the inner accumulators inside each (outer, inner) cell still ran
sequentially over a strided slice — both the mean accumulator and
the squared-deviation accumulator. For large reduce_size with
squared deviations (which double the relative error of each addend),
that drift becomes visible.

Fix: gather the strided values for each output cell into a reusable
scratch buffer (one allocation per call, not per cell), then
pairwise-sum (a) the mean computation and (b) the squared-deviation
sum using the existing `pairwise_sum_f64` and `pairwise_sum_map_f64`
helpers. The squared-deviation accumulator inherits the
O(log N · ε) bound from the helper instead of the prior
O(N · ε) sequential body. F32 sibling updated symmetrically.

Surface coverage:
  * `var_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64`
  * `var_dim_tensor_contiguous_f32`
  * `std_dim_tensor_contiguous_f64` (composes the above)
  * `std_dim_tensor_contiguous_f32` (composes the above)

Test: `var_dim_tensor_contiguous_pairwise_precision_at_large_n` runs
the variance of an arithmetic progression x_k = k for k in 0..100k
(analytical truth: N(N+1)/12 ≈ 8.33e8) and locks an absolute drift
< 1e-3, which the prior sequential inner-loop implementation would
have failed by ~3 orders of magnitude.

Quality gates: 12-crate workspace test sweep — ft-kernel-cpu 307 +
ft-api 1090 + ft-conformance 101 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-nn 150 +
ft-optim 74 + ft-data 103 + ft-serialize 15 + ft-runtime 2 +
ft-core 70 + ft-dispatch 9 + ft-device 6 — all green; clippy + ubs
clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
